findings.evidence: `CubeGenerator.lean:71-74` ignores `_hv`; existing unbundled lower API has `characteristicLowerFaceExponent (x) (S) (v)` with no membership at `CubeFace.lean:73-76`, its natCast proof uses only `Finset.erase_subset v S` at `CubeFace.lean:81-87`, and lower vertex/weight monotonicity uses `cubeVertices_subset`/`characteristicCubeWeight_mono` without membership (`CubeWeight.lean:133-138`, `CubeWeight.lean:243-246`).
findings.fix: Define `lowerFace (C : PlumbingCube V) (v : V)` with no proof, and drop `hv` from `lowerFace_mk`, `lowerFace_base`, `lowerFace_directions`, `vertices_lowerFace_subset`, `characteristicLowerFaceExponent`, `characteristicWeight_lowerFace_le`, and lower exponent zero/natCast statements; keep membership only on dimension-drop facts where `card_erase_of_mem` is needed.
findings.issue: `lowerFace` takes `_hv : v ∈ C.directions` even though the definition only uses `C.directions.erase v`, and downstream lower-face subset/weight/exponent facts are valid without membership.

summary: The bundled lower-face API is stricter than the existing unbundled API: it requires a membership proof even for operations and facts that are valid for arbitrary `v`. This should be generalized so callers do not have to carry unnecessary hypotheses.
